prompt: Join chassis_brake_validation_specs to vehicle_variant_configuration_specs. Which validation test has the shortest stop_distance_100_0_m? Return test_id, vehicle_variant, stop distance, body_style, trim_level, and curb_mass_kg.
Using the chassis table and the referenced vehicle variant row, identify the best 100-0 km/h braking result and include the variant's body style, trim, and curb mass.
For the chassis test with the minimum stopping distance, look up the linked vehicle configuration and report the test_id, variant_id, stop distance, trim_level, body_style, and curb_mass_kg.
Which chassis validation row is the braking-distance leader once vehicle_variant is resolved to the vehicle configuration table? Include the variant context from the vehicle row.
Resolve the shortest stop_distance_100_0_m chassis result back to its vehicle variant. What are the test id, variant id, stop distance, body style, trim level, and curb mass?

Among vehicle variants that appear in chassis_brake_validation_specs, which referenced variant has the highest tow_rating_kg? Return the chassis test_id, variant_id, tow rating, stop distance, body_style, and trim_level.
Join chassis tests to vehicle configurations and find the tested vehicle with the largest tow rating. Include its test id, stop distance, body style, and trim.
Considering only chassis-validated variants, identify the towing leader and report both chassis and vehicle fields needed to verify it.
Which chassis validation row references the highest-tow vehicle variant? Include test_id, vehicle_variant, tow_rating_kg, stop_distance_100_0_m, body_style, and trim_level.
Find the maximum tow_rating_kg after following chassis_brake_validation_specs.vehicle_variant into the vehicle table. What row and variant details identify it?

The dossier says variants with tow_rating_kg above 2000 must demonstrate stop_distance_100_0_m below 40 m. After joining chassis tests to vehicle variants, how many rows violate that rule, and which violating row has the worst stop distance?
Apply the >2000 kg towing / <40 m braking rule from main.md to the chassis-to-vehicle join. Return the violation count and the worst violating test with variant id, tow rating, and stop distance.
Using the vehicle tow ratings and chassis stop distances together, count validations where tow_rating_kg is over 2000 but stopping distance is not below 40 m. Include the worst case.
How many chassis validation rows fail the dossier's high-tow braking threshold, and what is the highest stop_distance_100_0_m among those failures? Return the row identifiers too.
Cross-check the towing threshold rule against the joined vehicle and chassis data. Report the count of failures and the worst failing test_id, variant_id, tow rating, and stop distance.

For variants with tow_rating_kg above 2000 that satisfy the dossier's below-40 m braking rule, which joined chassis row has the shortest stop distance? Include test_id, variant_id, tow rating, stop distance, body_style, and trim_level.
Among high-tow chassis validations that pass the <40 m requirement, identify the best braking result and include the linked vehicle context.
Filter the chassis-to-vehicle join to tow_rating_kg > 2000 and stop_distance_100_0_m < 40. Which row has the minimum stop distance?
Which validated high-tow vehicle is the strongest braking pass under the dossier threshold? Return the chassis id plus variant details.
Resolve chassis rows to vehicle variants, keep only towing ratings over 2000 kg with sub-40 m stops, and report the shortest stopping result.
The dossier says Sport trim variants must stop below 35 m. After joining chassis tests to vehicle variants, how many Sport rows fail that threshold, and which failure has the worst stop distance?
Apply the Sport trim braking rule from main.md to chassis validation rows by looking up each vehicle_variant. Return the failure count and worst failing test.
Using trim_level from the vehicle table and stop_distance_100_0_m from chassis tests, count Sport validations at 35 m or longer and identify the worst one.
How many joined chassis/vehicle rows violate the Sport below-35 m requirement, and what row is the maximum-distance violation?
Cross-reference chassis tests with vehicle trims to audit the Sport stopping-distance rule. Return the violation count plus the worst test_id and variant_id.
Which chassis validation tests share the maximum lateral_grip_g, and what trim_level and body_style do their referenced vehicle variants have?
Join the lateral-grip leaders in chassis_brake_validation_specs to the vehicle configuration table. Return all tied test ids, variant ids, trim levels, body styles, and the grip value.
For every row tied at the highest lateral_grip_g, resolve vehicle_variant and report the vehicle context from the variant table.
Identify all maximum lateral-grip chassis tests and include each linked vehicle's trim_level and body_style.
Report the chassis validation lateral-grip leaders together with their referenced vehicle variant details from vehicle_variant_configuration_specs.

The dossier says spring rates scale with curb_mass_kg plus max_payload_kg. In the chassis-to-vehicle join, which validation row has the largest curb+payload total, and what are its front and rear spring rates?
Join vehicle masses and payloads into chassis validation. Find the row with the maximum curb_mass_kg + max_payload_kg and return test_id, variant_id, total, components, and spring rates.
Which chassis validation references the vehicle with the highest loaded mass basis used for spring sizing? Include curb mass, payload, total, and both spring rates.
Using vehicle curb mass and max payload together with chassis spring-rate fields, report the maximum loaded-mass validation row.
Resolve chassis vehicle_variant values and compute curb_mass_kg + max_payload_kg. Which row is largest, and what front/rear spring rates are recorded?

Using the brake-energy basis in main.md and the chassis-to-vehicle join, which validated vehicle has the highest curb_mass_kg? Return test_id, variant_id, curb mass, stop distance, rotor sizes, and brake energy in MJ rounded to three decimals.
For the heaviest vehicle referenced by chassis validation, compute 0.5*m*v^2 at 100 km/h in MJ. Include the chassis row, variant id, stop distance, and front/rear rotor diameters.
Join chassis tests to vehicle curb masses and find the highest brake-energy case at 100 km/h. Report the identifiers, mass, energy in MJ, stop distance, and rotor sizes.
Which chassis-tested variant produces the largest single-stop kinetic energy using the dossier formula? Return the row and vehicle details needed to verify it.
Calculate the 100-0 km/h brake energy for the maximum-curb-mass validated variant, and include the chassis stop and rotor fields.

The dossier says leaf-solid configurations are mandatory for pickup variants with tow_rating_kg above 2000. In the chassis-to-vehicle join, how many pickup rows violate this, and what is the first listed violation?
Audit the pickup tow/axle rule by joining vehicle body_style and tow_rating_kg to chassis axle_config. Return the violation count and an example violating row.
Count joined rows where body_style is pickup, tow_rating_kg exceeds 2000, but axle_config is not leaf-solid. Include the first violation's test_id, variant_id, axle_config, and tow rating.
Using the axle rule in main.md, how many high-tow pickup chassis validations have a non-leaf-solid axle configuration? Provide a representative violating row.
Cross-reference pickup variants over 2000 kg tow rating with chassis axle_config. How many fail the leaf-solid requirement, and which row appears first?

The dossier says air-multilink suspensions pair with Executive and Premium trims. After joining chassis tests to vehicle trims, how many rows are air-multilink with Executive/Premium, and which of those has the highest stability_margin_pct?
Join axle_config from chassis validation with trim_level from vehicle variants. Count air-multilink rows on Executive or Premium trims and identify the stability-margin leader among them.
For air-multilink chassis rows whose referenced vehicle trim is Executive or Premium, return the row count and the maximum stability_margin_pct row.
Apply the air-multilink trim relationship from main.md to the joined chassis/vehicle data. What count matches Executive/Premium, and which row has the best stability margin?
Resolve chassis vehicle_variant references, filter air-multilink suspensions to Executive/Premium trims, and report the count plus highest stability margin row.

Main.md says each chassis validation test must reference a valid vehicle variant. How many chassis rows resolve to vehicle_variant_configuration_specs.variant_id, and how many invalid references are there?
Check referential integrity from chassis_brake_validation_specs.vehicle_variant to vehicle_variant_configuration_specs.variant_id. Return matched row count and invalid reference count.
Using the chassis table and vehicle table together, verify whether every chassis vehicle_variant has a corresponding variant_id. What are the valid and invalid counts?
Audit the chassis-to-vehicle reference described in the dossier. How many of the 1000 chassis rows join successfully, and how many fail to match?
Do all chassis validation records reference valid vehicle variants? Return the join key names and the counts of matching versus missing references.
Main.md says homologation_code prefixes must match the region field. In vehicle_variant_configuration_specs, how many variants violate that rule, and what is the first listed mismatch?
Using the homologation prefix rule from the dossier, count region/code prefix mismatches in the vehicle table and return the first mismatching variant.
Compare each vehicle row's region to the prefix of homologation_code. How many do not align, and which row is the first example?
Audit the region/homologation dependency stated in main.md. Report the mismatch count and first mismatch with variant_id, region, and homologation_code.
How many vehicle configurations have a homologation_code prefix that disagrees with region? Include the first row demonstrating the mismatch.
Main.md says lots achieve release_status=released only when battery_health_score_pct exceeds 96 and warranty_risk_index is below 2.0. How many released lots violate either threshold?
Apply the production release health/risk rule from the dossier to production_quality_measurements. Return the count of released lots that fail the thresholds.
For rows marked released, check battery_health_score_pct > 96 and warranty_risk_index < 2.0. How many exceptions are in the production table?
Using the release criteria from main.md, audit released production lots for threshold failures and report the violation count.
How many production lots with release_status released do not satisfy both the battery health and warranty risk requirements from the dossier?

Using the same production release thresholds from main.md, how many non-released lots still pass battery_health_score_pct > 96 and warranty_risk_index < 2.0? Include the first containment example and first hold example in file order.

Count production rows where release_status is not released but the health and warranty risk values satisfy the dossier's release thresholds. Return the first containment and first hold examples in file order.
Find non-released lots that meet battery_health_score_pct > 96 and warranty_risk_index < 2.0. How many are there, and what are the first containment and first hold rows in file order?
Apply the dossier's released-lot thresholds in reverse: among containment or hold lots, count those that pass both numeric gates and provide the first passing row for each status in file order.

Using main.md and production_quality_measurements, identify lots not marked released despite satisfying the battery health and warranty risk criteria. Return the count plus the first containment and first hold sample rows in file order.

Main.md says production build_date must follow calibration production_release_date. Compare the earliest production lot build date to the earliest calibration release date. Is any production lot earlier than the earliest calibration release, and which rows establish this?
Using production_quality_measurements and powertrain_calibration_specs, count lots with build_date before the earliest calibration production_release_date and report the earliest lot and calibration.
Cross-check the dossier's build-after-release concept at the dataset boundary: what is the earliest build, the earliest calibration release, and how many lots precede that release date?
Compare build_date values to the minimum production_release_date across calibrations. Return the count of production lots earlier than that minimum plus the lot and calibration identifiers.
Which production lot predates the earliest powertrain calibration release date, if any? Include the count of such lots and the relevant dates.

Main.md says high-output V50D calibrations require battery coolant flow above 15 L/min and ties coolant sizing to thermal_limit_deg_c. What is the highest-power V50D calibration? Return calibration_id, engine_family, peak_power_kw, thermal_limit_deg_c, how many battery packs exceed 15 L/min, and the max-coolant pack.

Using the V50D coolant-flow rule from the dossier, combine powertrain and battery data: identify the top V50D peak_power_kw row, including thermal_limit_deg_c, and count battery packs with coolant_flow_l_min > 15, including the maximum-flow pack.

For V50D calibration support, report the peak-power V50D calibration including thermal_limit_deg_c, plus the battery candidate count above the 15 L/min coolant-flow threshold. Include the highest coolant-flow battery.

Cross-reference main.md's high-output V50D cooling requirement with the powertrain and battery tables. Return the top V50D calibration including thermal_limit_deg_c, plus battery packs over 15 L/min and the max-flow pack.
Which V50D calibration is highest output, what is its thermal_limit_deg_c, and what battery-pack coolant-flow capacity exists above the dossier's 15 L/min requirement? Include count and leading pack.

Main.md says chassis tests with lateral_grip_g above 1.0 require NMC811 or NMC622 battery packs with peak_discharge_kw above 1500. How many high-grip chassis tests are there, and how many battery packs meet that chemistry/discharge condition by chemistry?
Using the high-grip battery support rule, count chassis rows with lateral_grip_g > 1.0 and count qualifying NMC811/NMC622 packs over 1500 kW, split by chemistry.
Combine chassis lateral-grip data with battery chemistry/discharge data. Return the number of tests above 1.0 g and the qualifying battery-pack totals for NMC811 and NMC622 above 1500 kW.
Audit the dossier's high-performance grip-to-battery rule. How many chassis tests trigger it, and how many NMC811/NMC622 packs satisfy the >1500 kW condition?
For lateral_grip_g greater than 1.0, summarize the chassis demand count and available qualifying battery packs by NMC811 versus NMC622 chemistry.

Main.md says NMC811 packs support Performance trim vehicles requiring peak_discharge_kw above 1000. How many Performance variants exist, how many NMC811 packs exceed 1000 kW, and which NMC811 pack has the highest peak discharge?
Combine the Performance-trim vehicle count with battery packs matching the NMC811 >1000 kW support rule. Return counts and the maximum-discharge NMC811 pack.
Using main.md's NMC811/Performance note, count Performance variants and qualifying NMC811 battery packs above 1000 kW, then identify the leading pack.
How many Performance vehicle configurations are in the vehicle table, and how many NMC811 packs provide more than 1000 kW peak discharge? Include the max-discharge NMC811 pack.
Cross-check Performance trim demand against NMC811 high-discharge battery supply using the dossier rule. Return the vehicle count, battery count, and top NMC811 pack.
Main.md says LFP packs are specified for Fleet trim variants. How many Fleet vehicle variants and LFP battery packs are present, and which LFP pack has the highest estimated_range_km?
Using the dossier's Fleet/LFP relationship, count Fleet trim variants and LFP packs, then report the longest-range LFP pack.
Combine vehicle trim data with battery chemistry data: how many Fleet variants are there, how many LFP packs exist, and which LFP pack reports the maximum estimated range?
For the LFP-to-Fleet specification note, summarize the Fleet variant count, LFP battery count, and range leader among LFP packs.
Check Fleet-trim configurations against available LFP battery specs. Return the counts and the highest-range LFP pack_id with estimated_range_km.
Main.md lists the six manufacturing plants. Using production_quality_measurements, which three plant_code values have the most production lots and what are their counts?
Using the plant codes described in the dossier and the production table, rank the top three plants by lot count.
Across the production quality records, which of the dossier's plant codes appear most often? Return the top three with counts.
Summarize production lot volume by plant_code and report the three highest-volume plants from the set listed in main.md.
Which manufacturing plants have the largest lot counts in production_quality_measurements? Use the plant-code context from main.md and return the top three.
